title: Arms open: I allow steady income
subtitle: A three-minute standing embodiment practice for opening the body to receive money.
excerpt: A three-minute standing embodiment practice for the body that braces for money to leave rather than opens to let it arrive. Arms open, palms forward, the receiving posture held for three minutes while a short statement repeats. Use when the feast-or-famine swing has been running or when the body feels tight around receiving.
sourceNotes: Open-posture embodiment practice is documented across somatic-therapy traditions (Peter Levine, Amy Cuddy's posture research, 2010 onward) and embodied affirmation practices. The steady-income application is adapted from Day 4 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "End the feast/famine cycle."

practice.whenToUse: Use in the morning to set a receiving posture for the day, or during a gap period when the body has been bracing for the famine after the feast.

content.text: A three-minute standing embodiment practice for the body that braces for money to leave rather than opens to let it arrive. Arms out, palms forward, the receiving posture held while a short statement repeats. The body learns the shape of receiving before the money has to prove it is safe. Use in the morning or during a gap period when the body is already bracing.
Stand. Feet hip-width apart, stable. Let the weight settle into the ground. One slow breath.

Open the arms out from the body — not a dramatic gesture, just enough to uncross the chest. Palms forward, fingers gently spread. This is the body's receiving shape. The closed, arms-in posture protects; the open posture allows.
Arms still open, say — out loud or in the mind:

attrs.quote: I allow steady income to arrive and stay.

content.text: Repeat three times, slow. Hold the open-arm position throughout. Notice what the body does with the words in this posture — whether the open arms make them easier to receive, or whether resistance comes. The resistance is useful data; let it be there while the arms stay open.
After the third repetition, let the arms return to the sides. Stay standing for one more slow breath. The practice is closed.

Open-posture embodiment practice is documented across somatic-therapy traditions and posture research (Amy Cuddy, 2010 onward). The steady-income application is adapted from Day 4 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), which addresses the feast-or-famine cycle and the nervous system's difficulty receiving money steadily.
